entry.title: Fly Client Proposal
entry.summary:
FlyClients present a significant advantage over ZK proofs due to their simplicity and ease of implementation, akin to SPV (Simplified Payment Verification) systems. This simplicity allows for easier reasoning and development from scratch, unlike ZK Starks, which are complex and unlikely to be fully proven end-to-end in the near future. The complexity of ZK Starks makes it challenging for a single individual to review thoroughly.
Moreover, the use of FlyClient as an opcode within Bitcoin's blockchain might offer a safer and more straightforward alternative compared to implementing ZK proofs for validating sidechains. The inherent simplicity and potential for safer deployment make FlyClient an appealing option that should not be dismissed prematurely in the context of Bitcoin development. This discussion highlights the ongoing exploration and debates within the Bitcoin Development community regarding optimal verification methods to enhance security and functionality in the blockchain ecosystem.
